explanation: Showroom price of the car. It has a strong positive effect; a more expensive base model leads to a higher resale price.
Age of the car. It has a strong negative effect; as the car gets older, it depreciates, decreasing the value.
Odometer reading. It has a negative effect; higher distance driven indicates more wear and tear, reducing value.
Diesel engine indicator. Diesel cars generally hold higher resale value and fuel efficiency.
Hyundai cars are feature-rich and hold standard to high resale value.
Toyota cars have excellent brand reputation and build quality, yielding superior resale values.
Feature that affects resale price of cars.
Automatic transmission indicator. Automatic cars command a price premium in the resale market.
Small engines represent budget commuter cars, keeping prices lower.
Feature that affects resale price of cars.
Honda cars have high reliability reputation, retaining value better than average.
Feature that affects resale price of cars.
Feature that affects resale price of cars.
Dealer seller. Dealers sell cars at a premium because they often include inspection/warranties.
Maruti Suzuki cars are popular with cheap maintenance, keeping demand and resale value stable.
